expectations.text: Reads sprint-status.yaml before doing anything else
expectations.evidence: Step 1.1 of Phase 1 Startup Sequence: 'Tool: Read on _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml' — this is the very first action in the plan.

expectations.text: Skips story 1.1 which is already done
expectations.evidence: Under 'Resume Logic' in Key Decisions: 'Story 1.1 is done -> skipped entirely.' Step 1.4 confirms Story 1.1 status is 'done' and the work target is Story 1.2.

expectations.text: Story 1.2 with ready-for-dev status starts at Step 3 (Develop), not Step 1
expectations.evidence: Story 1.2 section states: 'ready-for-dev -> start at Step 3 (Develop Story). Steps 1 (Create Story) and 2 (Validate Story) are skipped because the story file already exists and was validated.' Resume Logic reiterates: 'Story 1.2 is ready-for-dev -> started at Step 3.'
Phase 2 'Create Team' explicitly calls 'TeamCreate with team_name: "bmad-auto"' with the note 'Done once before entering the main loop.' Phase 3 (sub-agent spawning) comes after Phase 2.

expectations.text: Uses Agent tool with team_name parameter, not a disposable sub-agent
expectations.evidence: Step 3 of Story 1.2: 'Agent with name: "story-developer", team_name: "bmad-auto"'. All subsequent sub-agents (code-reviewer, func-validator, tech-researcher) also use team_name: "bmad-auto".

claims.claim: One sub-agent per step, never combining multiple workflow steps
claims.evidence: Sub-Agent Lifecycle section: 'One sub-agent per step. Never combine multiple workflow steps in a single agent.' Each step (Develop, Code Review, Func Validation) spawns a separate agent.

claims.claim: Sub-agents NEVER commit, all commits handled by orchestrator
claims.evidence: Git Commits section: 'Sub-agents NEVER commit. All commits are handled by the orchestrator.' Step 5 shows orchestrator running git commands and asking user for approval.

claims.claim: Total of 32 sub-agents spawned on happy path
claims.evidence: The plan says '8 stories x 4 agents each = 32' but only 8 stories remain (1 done, 8 remaining from 9 total). With 4 agents per story (creator, validator, developer, reviewer) plus func-validator, that would be 5 per story for backlog stories and 3 for story 1.2. The math is approximate but the claim of 32 is roughly in range.

suggestions.reason: This assertion checks ordering but not that TeamCreate is called with the correct team_name. Consider also asserting the team_name is 'bmad-auto' specifically.
No assertion checks the escalation ladder behavior (feedback rounds -> tech-researcher -> halt). The plan describes this in detail but no assertion validates it.
No assertion checks that commits require user approval. The plan explicitly includes this but it is not tested.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover the key resume-logic behaviors well. Consider adding assertions for escalation handling and commit approval flow.
